title: Innovative Light Italian Turkey Mince Pasta
description: A light and comforting Italian dish featuring turkey mince, sous-vide cooked to perfection, and served with a paleo-friendly pasta, this meal is a briny and satisfying option for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Preheat the sous-vide machine to 140 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the turkey mince, garlic, anchovy paste, salt, and black pepper, and mix well with your hands until just combined.
Form the turkey mixture into small meatballs and place them in a single layer in a sous-vide bag.
Seal the bag and cook the meatballs in the sous-vide machine for 1 hour.
While the meatballs are cooking, heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the cherry tomatoes to the skillet and cook, stirring occasionally, until they start to release their juices and soften, about 5 minutes.
Add the zucchini noodles to the skillet and cook, stirring occasionally, until they are slightly tender, about 3 minutes.
When the meatballs are done, remove them from the sous-vide bag and add them to the skillet with the tomatoes and noodles.
Stir in the fresh basil and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Serve the meatballs and noodles hot, garnished with additional basil if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
